meta_title: For Founders: design with AI to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Design with AI to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Overcome the founder's block of having no project ideas. Learn to design with AI using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your next SaaS MVP rapidly.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for a 'perfect' idea. Use AI to synthesize market gaps by analyzing high-churn SaaS categories. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid prototyping and Claude Code for iterative development, you can transform abstract pain points into functional, monetizable MVPs in hours rather than months. Build, validate, and pivot fast.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You’ve got the technical drive, but the 'no project ideas' paralysis is keeping your potential locked behind a wall of indecision. Most founders fail not because they lack skill, but because they spend months hunting for a unicorn idea instead of shipping a functional solution.

Design with AI is your force multiplier. We aren't just generating code; we are architecting business logic. By integrating Antigravity for design-to-code iteration and Claude Code for intelligent scaffolding, you can treat your project list like a high-frequency trading desk: test, iterate, and kill the losers early.

steps.title: Niche Discovery via Sentiment Analysis
steps.description: Identify a high-friction problem by scraping Reddit or G2 reviews. Use AI to find where users are complaining about existing software.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a market researcher. Analyze these 50 user complaints from G2 regarding project management software and suggest 3 high-impact, low-complexity features that solve the top recurring pain point.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'analyze niche trends and create a markdown report of the top 3 SaaS opportunities'

steps.title: Rapid UI Architecture
steps.description: Translate your chosen idea into a high-fidelity visual structure using Antigravity to generate the boilerplate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a clean, conversion-focused landing page and dashboard layout for a B2B SaaS tool that helps freelancers automate invoice follow-ups. Use a modern, minimalist Tailwind CSS aesthetic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --path ./src/components --prompt 'scaffold the dashboard UI based on the Antigravity design'

steps.title: Database Schema Generation
steps.description: Define your data model to ensure your MVP handles users and core entities correctly from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for a SaaS MVP tracking invoice status, user authentication, and client data. Provide the SQL or Prisma schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate prisma schema and set up database migrations based on the architectural plan'

steps.title: Core Logic Implementation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write the backend services and API routes that power your primary value proposition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the server-side logic for an automated email follow-up function using Node.js and a mock email service API.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file src/services/email.ts --instruction 'implement the automated email trigger logic with error handling'

steps.title: Validation & Deployment
steps.description: Prepare your MVP for public feedback by configuring a deployment pipeline.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a deployment script and environment variable configuration guide for a standard Vercel deployment of this SaaS project.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure github actions for automated testing and deployment'

pro_tips: Prioritize 'boring' problems over 'innovative' ones; boring problems have customers with existing budgets.
Ship your MVP without a database first using local storage or a flat file to validate the UI flow in under 4 hours.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests before you write the actual logic to force yourself to define clear business requirements.
Always build with a 'kill switch'—ensure your code is modular enough to scrap the backend and keep the frontend if the pivot is required.
Focus on the 'Time-to-First-Value' metric; if a user doesn't hit your core feature within 60 seconds, your design is too complex.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, spend 48 hours in cold outreach. If you don't get at least 5 signups or meaningful feedback, use Claude Code to refactor your value proposition and redeploy. Speed is your only competitive advantage.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to turn 'no ideas' into a live, scalable product. Stop analyzing and start building the future you want to see. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
